There is no try-with-resources or finally block to guarantee the stream is closed across all execution paths (normal return or exception handling), so the resource is not released before method exit.
(2) Second line: Answer: Yes

Its release is not ensured on all execution paths, so the resource may stay unclosed.

87.explanation:
(1) Explanation: The lock resource is acquired at line 11 of the method, and the corresponding unlock only runs on the normal execution path at line 23. If an exception occurs between the lock and unlock steps, the unlock will not execute, so the lock is not released on all exits from the method.
(2) Answer: Yes

88.explanation:
(1) First line: Explanation: The ZipFile resource is declared in a try-with-resources statement, which guarantees automatic closure when the try block exits via any path including normal flow, exceptions, or early breaks, so the resource is definitely released before method exit.
(2) Second line: Answer: No

Fallback: no-sink/weak-release branch is treated as leak-reachable.

90.explanation:

Releases only occur on inner try exceptions or when acceptNewConnection is false, not across all valid execution paths, so the resource is not definitely released before method exit.
